pi_items.source: setup_opensis.sh and direct_db_setup.sh, also export_result.sh - all use DB_USER='opensis_user'
setup_opensis.sh and direct_db_setup.sh, also export_result.sh - all use DB_PASS='opensis_password_123'
setup_opensis.sh and direct_db_setup.sh - both create and use DB_NAME='opensis'
setup_opensis.sh line 172: INSERT INTO login_authentication (user_id, profile_id, username, password...) VALUES (1, 1, 'admin'...)
setup_opensis.sh lines 157-158 and direct_db_setup.sh lines 65-68 - both use 'Admin@123' as the password for admin user

Actual OpenSIS V9.2 schema from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OS4ED/openSIS-Classic/master/install/OpensisSchemaMysqlInc.sql shows report_card_comments table has columns: id, syear, school_id, course_id, sort_order, title, last_updated, updated_by. NO 'code' field exists.

pi_items.metadata_value: Task expects to create comments with code 'PLE' and 'MHW' with titles and sort_order
pi_items.verified_value: Field 'code' does not exist in schema; task scripts attempt to query SELECT title, code, sort_order from report_card_comments WHERE code IN ('PLE', 'MHW')
pi_items.source: export_result.sh line 33 queries non-existent 'code' field; OpenSIS schema verification shows this field is not part of standard schema
Task metadata expects this as title for code 'PLE', but cannot be verified without working code field
Task metadata expects this as title for code 'MHW', but cannot be verified without working code field; however 'missing homework' is a standard educational report card comment topic
Would be sort_order for 'PLE' comment, but cannot verify without code field
Would be sort_order for 'MHW' comment, but cannot verify without code field

privileged_info_summary: The OpenSIS environment uses verified credentials: db_user=opensis_user, db_pass=opensis_password_123, db_name=opensis (from setup scripts). Admin login is admin/Admin@123. OpenSIS version is 9.2. However, the task expects to manipulate a 'code' field in the report_card_comments table that does not exist in the standard OpenSIS V9.2 schema. The actual schema includes columns: id, syear, school_id, course_id, sort_order, title, last_updated, updated_by. The task setup and verification scripts attempt to query this non-existent 'code' field, which represents a critical schema mismatch between the task specification and actual OpenSIS database structure.
